title_zh: 用 max bid limit 修剪异常高价 click 而非压住整盘出价
summary_zh: Smart Bidding 大多数时候比人工好,但偶尔会在个别 auction 出爆炸 CPC(平均 2 块的词被拍到 20 块)。Andrew 的最常用打法是把 max bid 设在 1.2-1.8 倍平均 CPC,只削掉最贵的尖峰,不影响中低价 auction。这与 seasonal bid adjustment 的等比例下调不同——后者会让本来就低的出价掉出 auction、丢量;max bid 只压顶,数据完整,适合表现差时给算法降速但不锁死。
quote_en: Paying $20 for a keyword that normally is $2 in average cost per click is just wasteful. The way that I use max bidding limits the most is when I encounter poor performance. I just put a max bid limit. Maybe I put it at 1.5x the average CPC.

title_zh: max bid 设太狠会触发死亡螺旋,3 个月后利润绝对值反掉
summary_zh: 测试在多个国家/campaign 同时压 1x 平均 CPC 的激进 max bid:前 2 个月 click 只掉 15%、利润率涨 15-30% 看起来很美;但 conversion 量掉 33%,被锁出最优质 auction。3 个月后进入下行螺旋,Google 端和后端数据同时变差,即使 ROAS 漂亮,绝对利润也开始下滑。激进压价只能短期(过完 BFCM 时)用,长期必死。
quote_en: We lost approximately 15% of clicks which doesn't seem like that much because we saw a 15 to 30% better profit. However, and this is was the key thing, conversions dropped by 33%. What this means is that we're getting locked out of the best auctions.

title_zh: 拒绝信 Google rep:limited by bid limit 不等于丢 76% auction
summary_zh: Google rep 常拿 Smart Bidding 报告里的 limited by bid limit 76% 来劝你松开限制,声称你丢了 76% 的 auction。Andrew 实测多年:max bid 真实含义只是 76% 的 auction 里 Smart Bidding 想出更高价但被你压住,不是被踢出。但 minimum bid limit 才是真正会让你直接掉出 auction 的设置——文档没写,实操中拿掉 min bid 反而流量上涨。结论:听 rep 话之前先看自己后台数据。
quote_en: What it means is that in 76% of the auctions that you have been in, smart bidding could have been bidding higher for it. It doesn't mean that you've dropped out of 76% of the auctions you could have been in completely.

title_zh: 接手老账户先批量删 negative keywords,5/8 案例后表现反而提升
summary_zh: 很多 agency 留下的老账户里堆了大量 negative keywords(尤其是 keyword sculpting / cannibalization 那一套互相排斥的列表)。Andrew 的 Savvy Revenue 过去一年接手的 8 个账户里有 5 个直接清光所有 negative,全部表现优于以往;有家从某 agency 拿过来的客户连续多次复现同一结论,year-over-year 涨 50%。逻辑:keyword 之所以走到 DSA/别的 campaign 是因为 ad rank/quality score 不够,你强行加 negative 等于把它彻底压没。删除时配合略高 budget(+20%) 和 max CPC limits 防止失控,或一周删 10-20% 慢慢释放。
quote_en: The amount of accounts that we've taken in the last year maybe five and again we don't take more than eight accounts in a year maybe five five of those eight have all had all negative negative keywords removed and all have outperformed after.

title_zh: 看 search term match type 报告,而不是 keyword match type
summary_zh: 大部分人看 broad/phrase/exact 表现是按 keyword match type 来汇总,会得出 broad 花了 30-40% 预算 ROAS 还可以的结论。但同样数据切成 search term match type(用户实际搜索词跟你的 keyword 是 exact / close variant / broad 哪种关系)往往会发现 60-80% 的 broad keyword 花费实际命中的还是 exact 搜索词——也就是说就算关掉 broad,你也能拿到同样的搜索量。要判断 broad 到底带没带新增量,必须再下钻到 campaign 级,看哪些 campaign 的 broad ROAS 是 1x 这种级别。
quote_en: When you actually look at, okay, if you didn't have broad match you would have hit 90% of the same search terms anyways. In this case, it's performing like we want. Some campaigns the ROAS was like 1x on broad match. So really removing it in those cases made sense.

title_zh: 搜索量饱和后再加预算只会推高 CPC,要承认天花板
summary_zh: 客户老说要 grow,加预算却只换来 CPC 上涨没新增 conversion——先去看 search impression share 这一列。如果非品牌词都已经 90% 了,意味着真实世界搜你这个东西的人你已经覆盖完了,smart bidding exploration / broad match / AI Max 都救不了你,因为没有新搜索可以挖。这时不是去乱开自动化,而是要承认天花板,告诉客户:要么扩 SKU/品类、要么换渠道(Demand Gen / 站外),或者进入 maintenance 模式降 fee 等下一波增长机会。这是顾问最被低估的能力——如实说话。
quote_en: We added the search impression share column. It was 90% search impression share for non-brand search. You have reached all the people searching. There are real people in the real world who are typing things into Google and you're reaching them all. There is no money to spend here.

title_zh: 用 holdout / 地区分组测 brand 词的增量,而不是看 ROAS
summary_zh: brand bidding 该不该投不存在通用答案,要看 incrementality:这笔 conversion 不投 brand 词会不会照样发生。最干净的做法是按地理区分两组,一组开 brand 一组关,看后端总 conversion 差多少;次选是停一周开一周交替几次。直接看 brand campaign 的 8x/20x ROAS 没意义——那是用户本来就要找你的钱。也千万不要混 new vs existing customer 那个维度进来,那是另一个问题。
quote_en: Instead of asking, should I be bidding for my brand? The better question to ask is, well, is brand bidding incremental for me? Would the conversion have happened anyway? And the only way to actually test this is to do a holdout test.

title_zh: brand 词用手动出价更划算,smart bidding 的优势在 brand 上失效
summary_zh: Smart bidding 厉害的场景是大量 keyword、转化率波动大、竞争多——brand 词全反过来:几个词、转化率稳定、几乎没竞争。这种场景下 smart bidding 会把 CPC 报到远高于必要值。Andrew 用 experiment 把 brand bid 直接砍 50% 测试,impression / click / revenue 都没掉,直接省一半成本,统计显著。所以大品牌账户上 brand 词省一半 CPC 完全可能,只是大家没去测。例外是 brand shopping campaign,SKU 多+和经销商抢流量,smart bidding 才有意义。
quote_en: Big brands can save big bucks on shaving off half their CPCs and have no impact on clicks whatsoever. Smart bidding works great when there's a lot of competitors per keyword. However, for branded keywords, the opposite is the case. You have very few competitors, very stable conversion rates, five keywords, 10, 20 keywords tops.

title_zh: brand 必须从 PMax 排除,否则 PMax 就是把钱往 brand 词倒
summary_zh: 90% 的 DTC 品牌从 Meta 转到 Google 后说 Google 不行,不是 Google 不行,是因为没把 brand term 从 PMax 里排除。一旦月预算到 1-5 万美金,PMax 会把绝大部分预算自动倒进 brand 词,然后看起来 ROAS 很漂亮但其实零增量。规则:有点知名度的品牌一律 exclude;只有刚起步、几乎没人搜你的新品牌可以不管。同时 brand vs non-brand 的 ROAS 报表必须分开,绝对不要合并——合并后没人能为非品牌负责。
quote_en: The reason why 90% of DTC brands can't get Google ads to work is because they don't exclude brand terms when they start spending $10,000, $50,000 a month. The majority goes into brand terms in PMAX. If you're an existing brand, for goodness sake, exclude.

title_zh: shopping feed 用 item_group_id 把变体聚合,不要瞎删 variant
summary_zh: 网上很多人说 shopping feed 要砍 variant 简化数据,Andrew 反对:多数情况砍 variant 反而伤覆盖,因为只留一个尺码/颜色的话只要那个 SKU 缺货整个 product 在 Google 就不展示了。基础动作只有一个——给所有同款变体打 item_group_id,Google 自然会聚合数据。只有当一个产品超过 15 个 variant、且 smart bidding 数据被打散到没法分析时,才考虑去重。即便去重也要保留有人搜的差异维度(颜色/材质),并且写好 fallback 规则:首选 variant 缺货时自动切到有货的同组变体,否则会被判 disapproved。
quote_en: If you're only advertising for the black size large t-shirt and it goes out of stock even though all other sizes are available then Google will not show anything. The simple thing whenever you work with product variants, this is not rocket science, you add the item group ID to group similar variants together.

title_zh: 测试 feed 改动只看 traffic 是否稳,不要纠结 ROAS / impression share
summary_zh: 做 feed 层面的改动(去重 variant、改 title、调 attribute)时,大量人盯着 ROAS 和 conversion rate 慌着回滚——这是错的,smart bidding 还没学过来这些指标没意义。Andrew 的判断标准只有一个:traffic 是否稳定。流量没掉就继续,流量明显掉立即回滚。先在 20% 的 SKU 上小范围跑,traffic 没异常再扩到全量。impression share 这种指标在多 SKU 跨产品比对里噪音太大,不要看。
quote_en: If you got a big catalog test for goodness sakes test this on 20% of products first. Don't obsess over conversion rates or ROAS during testing because it will not have that big of an impact on smart bidding's algorithms initially. The goal is if traffic stays the same because we can track that really really easily then that's the goal.

title_zh: 全部塞 PMax 那一套是把 search/shopping 用同一个 ROAS 目标喂
summary_zh: Google rep 一直推 consolidate 到一个 PMax 让 AI 分配预算,Andrew 拉数据直接打脸:同一个账户 search 和 shopping 的最优 ROAS 目标本就不同——shopping 通常能撑更高 ROAS,search 给同样目标会被压到只占 5% 预算(健康水位是 20-28%)。这意味着 PMax 一刀切的 ROAS 让 search 永远跑不出量。要 grow 必须拆开:search 用自己的目标和 max CPC,shopping 用自己的;其次 bestseller / 不盈利 / longtail 三档产品也不该共用 ROAS。
quote_en: Some accounts run search and shopping where 5% of the spend goes into search where others are at a much more healthy 28%, 20%, 22%. Shopping will routinely be able to produce a higher ROAS than search. So setting the same ROAS target is insufficient for growth.

title_zh: PMax 的 display/video 不是上漏斗流量,而是 retargeting
summary_zh: 很多人以为开 PMax 等于自动跑上漏斗 awareness,因为里面包了 display 和 video。Andrew 反对:PMax 的 display 和 video 跟 search/shopping 共用同一个 ROAS 目标,要在那个 ROAS 下达标只能跑 retargeting,push 渠道≠上漏斗。所以指望 PMax 帮你做 awareness 然后回流到 search,逻辑根本不成立。PMax 唯一真正的好处是:聚合数据给 smart bidding 学习 + 让没跑过 DSA 的账户多吃 10-30% 搜索流量。
quote_en: This assumption is a very common misunderstanding. So in order to hit the same ROAS target for display as you're doing on search, then you need to do retargeting. So just because we're it's push advertising like display video doesn't mean we're actively targeting people at the top of the funnel.

title_zh: PMax 性能掉了你没工具能修——要预留 standalone 框架
summary_zh: PMax 跑得好的时候没人在意黑盒,但常见周期是 3/6/12/18 个月后开始 stagnate 或下滑。这时候 PMax 给你的杠杆只有改预算、改 ROAS 目标、调 asset group 这几个伪动作,没法定位是 brand / category / money keyword 哪一层在垮、是 search 还是 shopping 在掉。standalone search + standalone shopping(每个 bid strategy 至少 300 转化/月)虽然门槛高,但能 (1) 跑 ad/landing page 测试 (2) 给不同 keyword 设不同 ROAS 目标 (3) 出问题时定位修复。所以最好早期就把核心 SKU 拆出来 standalone,别全压 PMax。
quote_en: Everything is good until it's not good anymore. And that's really the biggest issue with PMax and consolidating everything. You don't have a lot of options to actually optimize it because you can kind of change the budget, but that doesn't do much. Then you can change the ROAS target up or down. What does that solve?

title_zh: e-com 默认用 target ROAS,不要用 maximize conversion value
summary_zh: Maximize Conversion Value 听起来更聪明:给个预算,Google 自由发挥。但 Google 上的搜索量天天波动,这种模式会强迫算法在 demand 低的日子加价烧完预算(CPC 涨 → ROAS 掉),在 demand 高的日子反而不敢加 budget(CPC 被压低 → 错过红利,revenue 被锁顶)。Target ROAS 是反过来:只认每次 click 的合理价格,demand 高就放量,demand 低就少花,ROAS 稳定。结论:e-com 默认 target ROAS,maximize conversion value 只在三种特殊情况用——老板按预算考核、需要花量、或重置账户。
quote_en: Telling Google to spend the same thing every day, no matter how much demand there is, makes little sense. On days or weeks with low demand, you'll start seeing smart bidding spend your budget by increasing the CPC. Target ROAS doesn't care if there's a thousand people searching or 10,000 people searching in a day.

title_zh: 用 portfolio bid strategy 把多 campaign 合并喂数据
summary_zh: smart bidding 数据越多反应越快、wasted spend 和 opportunity cost 都更低——但前提是数据合并到同一个 bid strategy 学习。Andrew 的做法是用 Portfolio Bid Strategy(过去叫 Bidding Strategy 现在又改名)把 40 个 search campaign 串到同一个策略里,顺便能拉出 projected ROAS / 实际 ROAS / max CPC / min CPC 几个关键报表来诊断。例如近 7 天 actual ROAS 514 但 projected 只到 571,远低于目标 676 → 立刻知道要调 max CPC 或 seasonal bid adjustment,不是干等。
quote_en: We have 40 campaigns all lumped together, which means that we give more data to smart bidding. The key point why more data works better is that smart bidding can then react faster to changes in ROAS, which means less wasted ad spend and less opportunity cost.

title_zh: 降 ROAS 目标 = 让算法去探索,升 ROAS 目标 = 收紧
summary_zh: target ROAS 不是设完就不动,而要在节点上手动调:每次降 ROAS 你是在告诉 smart bidding 去探索新流量、买更多 click;每次升 ROAS 是让它收紧、变高效。即便全年要稳定 ROAS,在大促前/大促刚开始降一档目标,能拿到几天甚至几周的高量低价红利,等算法反应过来调高时已经吃到肉。日常不要频繁动,但 peak / off-peak 转换、conversion 异常、跑量目标变化时一定要主动调。
quote_en: Every single time you lower your ROAS target, you're telling smart bidding to go explore, find more volume, buy me more traffic. Decreasing your target ahead of a peak season or right in the beginning of peak season can help you dramatically increase your volume.

title_zh: brand 词用 manual bid 而非 smart bidding,CPC 能砍到 1/10
summary_zh: smart bidding 的设计目标是用 ROAS 内最大化转化量,不是最低成本拿同一笔转化。所以投 brand 词用 smart bidding 等于主动告诉 Google:再多花点也行。Andrew 给两类账户分别跑 manual bid 测试:无外部分销(没有经销商/marketplace 抢流量)的品牌直接砍到 CPC 降 90%、conversion 不变;有外部分销的(经销商/Amazon 在抢)需要更谨慎,但 manual + 实验仍能省 50%。如果 SKU 太多,branded shopping 可以用 smart bidding,但必须配 max CPC 和很高的 ROAS 目标。
quote_en: After switching to manual bids, the cost per conversion dropped by 10x. Smart bidding maximizes for volume, not for efficiency. Even with a high ROAS target, smart bidding won't minimize spend if it can get more conversions or more exposure at a lower efficiency.

title_zh: 用两个 benchmark 判断 brand 是否过投:>10% spend 或 brand ROAS < 2× 非品牌
summary_zh: 怎么知道 brand 词花多了?Andrew 给两个粗暴 benchmark:(1) brand campaign 占总广告预算 >10% 是大红灯;(2) brand ROAS < 非品牌 ROAS 的 2 倍是另一红灯。比如非品牌 ROAS 500%,brand 应该至少 800-1000%,健康水位通常是 8-10x 起,有的行业能到 20-40x。如果你的 brand ROAS 只比非品牌高一点点,要么 brand 出价太狠,要么非品牌太怂。验证方法是用 Google Ads 的 Experiment 功能:50/50 split,把 max CPC 砍一半看 click 是否稳、cost 是否降,稳的话就保留新值,继续往下压。
quote_en: If you're spending more than 10% of your overall ad budget on your brand, that's a huge red flag. If your branded ROAS is less than two times of your generic ROAS, then it's another red flag. Normally your ROAS in your brand especially in search want to be at least 8 to 10x and probably 20, 30, 40x.

title_zh: brand 不要追求第一名:从 99% 掉到 95% 但 CPC 砍半 = 大胜
summary_zh: owner 心理上很难接受自己品牌词搜出来不是 #1,但实际算账:top impression share 从 99% 降到 95%、CPC 直接砍 50%、conversion 只丢 5-15%——这是大胜利。'坚持 #1 brand 词'是给 Google 交免费税,真正得利的是看着你出价撑高自己词价的竞争对手。在欧洲还可以走 gentleman's agreement,跟竞品/经销商互相不投对方品牌词;美国不流行但仍可试。
quote_en: If you drop from 99% the top impression share to 95% and you save 50% of your CPCs, you might look at it like 'Oh my god, we're not number one.' But if you save 50% of your CPCs and you lose 5%, 10%, 15% of your conversions, that is a win. Over bidding on your name is like giving Google free tax.

title_zh: smart bidding 时代 negative keyword 是按 search-term 自动压价不是死刑
summary_zh: 5 年前 negative keyword 的逻辑是 keyword 级别一刀切,但 smart bidding 已经在 search-term 级别按 device / 价格区间 / 不同 product 单独调价了——同一个搜索词在 desktop 不赚钱可能 mobile 赚,在 $1 CPC 不赚但 $0.25 赚。你提前手动 negate 等于把这个细颗粒度优化的能力锁死。Andrew 的判断:除了明显无关词(竞品名、完全错产品)和 6-12 个月持续表现差的低量词,其余别动,让算法自己降价。新建账户初期或没数据时再加。
quote_en: It already acts like a negative keyword system, but with more data and faster action time and more actions than we will have. A search term might lose money at a $1 cost per click, but it will kill it. It will perform excellently at 25 cents. Smart bidding handles that nuance.

title_zh: 用 click bucket 分箱再加 negative,而不是一刀切 ROAS<200%
summary_zh: Andrew 的标准 negative keyword 工作流:把所有 search term 拉出来按 click 数分箱(0-10 / 10-20 / 20-30 / 30-50 / 50+),自己手算 ROAS(不要用 Google 给的聚合 ROAS,均值的均值会骗你)。0-10 click 的低量段不能动——因为里面同时有未来高 ROAS 和低 ROAS 的种子,只要再多一个 click 转化它就跳到高 ROAS bucket。真正能 negate 的只是 20+ click 还低 ROAS 的部分,实操中能砍掉的浪费 spend 通常是 4-8%——别指望靠 negative 大幅省钱,主要价值是清干净不该出的词。
quote_en: If I take out these three buckets, meaning everything over 20 clicks, then I find 24,000 in spend that I can remove. That's very little. It's like 3 and a half % of the total spend. We find out that roughly between four to 8% is the actual spend we can remove by adding negative keywords.

title_zh: 维护两个 negative 列表:evergreen 和 low-season,大促前清掉 low-season
summary_zh: negative keyword 不要堆在一个列表里。分两份:evergreen(永远不想出现的烂词)和 low-season(本季度不行但旺季可能爆)。比如卖泳衣在二月加 negative 的某些词,到 5-7 月旺季会反过来转化,旺季前要主动从 list 里移除。Andrew 还建议:每年至少一次完全清空 negative keyword 让 smart bidding 重新跑 4 周,因为 SKU/品类/价格/竞争都在变,去年烂词今年可能赚——别让 2018 年的 negative 列表把你账户卡死。
quote_en: Have two negative keyword lists. Evergreen is the horribly performing. Low season is if you're selling swimsuits and then in February some search terms might not work, but in May, June, July they're going to perform exceptionally well. Put them on a low season negative keyword list that you can remove when you get into your peak season.

title_zh: 看 hidden vs visible search term 必须先剔除 brand,否则结论是假的
summary_zh: 有篇 viral 文章说 Google 通过 hidden search term 偷广告主钱:visible 5.6x ROAS,hidden 才 2.59x。Andrew 拆原文数据:差距全来自 brand——brand 词天然不涉及隐私所以 100% 可见、ROAS 也高;hidden 那一坨里非品牌占比远高于 visible,把品牌这块差距摊到 hidden 头上,看起来就像 hidden 在亏钱。一旦把数据切成 'non-brand only',visible 和 hidden ROAS 几乎一致。教训:任何按 visible/hidden、search-partner、device 分组的报表都得先拆 brand 和 non-brand,否则品牌的极端 ROAS 会污染所有结论。
quote_en: When non-brand, this is where it starts being interesting. There's no difference whatsoever. Branded terms are more visible because there's no privacy issues with that. So we're having a bigger portion of the branded search terms that are visible versus non-brand where there's a bigger portion that are hidden.

title_zh: 粒度切太细会饿死 smart bidding,简单结构反而跑得更好
summary_zh: DTC 账户最常见错误是把 campaign 拆得太细——按 category、按 brand vs non-brand、按 phrase vs broad,搞出 18+ 个 campaign。这种粒度等于把数据切碎,smart bidding 没有足够样本就学不会:出价不准、RSA 选不出最优 headline、product-keyword 匹配也乱。Andrew 实操偏好的 baseline 结构:bestseller campaign + main campaign + push campaign(给新品/新品类喂量),最多再加一两个迁就客户的特殊 campaign。复杂度迟早会自己长出来——别一开始就搭复杂结构,'感觉略简单'就停手。
quote_en: Granularity can kill automation. The more you split up the campaigns, the more you split up the data. The more you split up the data, the longer it takes to use the data. Every setup should have a feeling of being this is a little bit too simple. Not overly simple, but just on the verge of I could tweak this. And that's where you stop.

title_zh: 诊断 smart bidding 看 projected ROAS 不看 actual ROAS
summary_zh: Smart bidding 出价不是按 actual ROAS 决策的,是按它自己预测的 projected ROAS。所以你看到 actual 68%(目标 661%) 算法却没降价时,别骂它瞎,因为有 conversion lag 它知道还有转化在路上。判断方法:在 bid strategy 报表里 hover ROAS 列,看 projected ROAS。例如 actual 68 但 projected 677、目标 661,说明算法觉得已经达标甚至超标,继续维持出价是对的。CPC 也不是好指标——smart bidding 会在不赚钱的 segment 上把人踢出 auction、在赚钱的 segment 上保持高 CPC,平均 CPC 看不出任何东西。
quote_en: Projected ROAS is your core KPI to understand what it's doing. The actual ROAS is what the ROAS is with all the traffic that's come in. But if you hover over it, you can see what is it predicting will come in further. CPC is one of the worst things you can look at when you figure out is my bidding changes working or not.

title_zh: 上限突破靠 off-platform:价格、产品页、AOV 比 broad-vs-PMax 重要
summary_zh: PPC specialist 的真正价值不是切 broad/exact,而是看出 off-platform 的瓶颈帮客户提天花板。三个真实案例:(1) 客户改对价格策略,Q1 conversion rate 从 1.32% 涨到 3.34%,profit 直接起飞;(2) 'member price' ROAS 一直差,根因是 product page 没说清如何成为会员、客户不信折扣是真的——改文案就能修;(3) 频繁促销让 AOV 大幅波动,smart bidding 学不会出价节奏,建议直接减促销次数。能跟客户讲清楚这些价值,比讲 'PMax vs standard' 之类账户内话题更值钱。
quote_en: Off-platform optimization more or less drove the entire conversion rate increase. We saw that example before where we just got way higher 3x the conversion rate by working on the prices. It doesn't all have to be broad match. It doesn't all have to be a PMax versus standard shopping conversation. This is what clients are looking for.

title_zh: 拆 campaign 看 conversion 数不看 spend,门槛 300 单/月
summary_zh: 看到一份 case study 说月花 5,000 美金就开始拆 top performing categories 进独立 campaign,Andrew 直接打 1/10 分。拆分的判断标准从来不是 ad spend,是 conversion 数。低于 300 单/月就拆,smart bidding 没有足够数据,每个新 campaign 都会 underperform。一个允许的例外:把表现差但吃量的 SKU 从主 campaign 抽出来扔到次级 campaign 限制其 spend,这才叫 segmentation,不是简单按品类切。Amazon 上同理:Sponsored Display/Brand 单独 campaign 之前先看每个 ASIN 的转化体量。
quote_en: I like doing segmentation, anybody who follow me know that that's one of the core things is to split up. But not until you get over what I call 300 conversions a month, and these guys had 395 conversions in total. This overall segmentation used in this particular case I will give it a 1 out of 10.

title_zh: 用 add-to-cart 当 proxy 转化前必须按比例缩 conversion value
summary_zh: 高 AOV、低转化体量、长决策周期的产品,可以把 add-to-cart 设为 conversion 给算法喂数据。但绝大多数人忽略一个关键步骤:必须按 ATC→购买的比例倒推缩小 ATC 的 conversion value。如果 5 次 ATC 才有 1 次成交,那 ATC 的 value 应设成实际订单价值的 20%(1/5)。不缩 value,直接拿购物车价当转化值,后期切回 purchase conversion 时所有历史 ROAS baseline 全错,smart bidding 学到的出价策略也是垃圾。Amazon 站内拿不到 ATC 信号,但思路通用——任何代理转化都要按转化漏斗比例缩 value。
quote_en: What you want to do is you want to try to calculate what is the relationship from add to cart to purchase. So if it takes five add to carts to result in one conversion or one purchase, then you want to reduce the value of the add to cart conversion by 5x, meaning 20% of what an actual add to cart value is.

title_zh: 判定 case study 真假先验 platform ROAS 与总 revenue 的相关性
summary_zh: 看别人晒的增长 case 时,Andrew 的第一道筛子是查 platform 内 ROAS 与总营收的相关性。一份案例显示 Google Ads ROAS 从 5x 跳到 10x 但同期 blended revenue 没动,另一段 ROAS 翻倍而 blended 反而走平,这种背离说明数字编了或漏掉了其他渠道变化没说。真增长的特征:platform ROAS 和 blended 同方向同幅度变动。Amazon 投放复盘也一样——Sponsored ads 报表里的 sales 涨了,但店铺总销售没涨,要么是 cannibalization 自然单,要么是数据错位,绝不是真正的增量。
quote_en: In order to verify that, you need to make sure that the relationship between Google Ads revenue and the total revenue is there. In this case, we're seeing that the ROAS in Google Ads is one and then it's 18 and here is 40 and here's 50. Then in October, Google Ads ROAS goes down to 5x. Blended is up at 50. There's no correlation.

title_zh: PMax→Standard 切换前必看 channel breakdown 和 100 单门槛
summary_zh: 从 PMax 切回 Standard Shopping 之前先做三个检查:(1) 拉 channel breakdown,看 conversion 来自 shopping 还是 search/YouTube/display——如果 shopping 干了大头,放心切;如果 search 占比高,得单独建 search 或 DSA campaign 接住;(2) 月转化低于 100 单不要切,smart bidding 没数据训练,切完只会更糟;(3) 想切的真实理由必须是'shopping 表现好但 PMax 不愿意 scale 这个 channel',不是凭感觉。Amazon 同理:看 Sponsored Brands vs Display vs SP 的 attribution 占比再决定要不要拆 campaign。
quote_en: If shopping is doing all the heavy lifting, then you're probably fine to switch. You're not probably fine, you're definitely fine to switch. If you're getting fewer than 100 conversions per month inside of PMAX, be careful. If you're under 30, you're basically flying blind trying to make the switch.

title_zh: 切到 standard shopping 前先排除品牌词解锁真实非品牌 ROAS
summary_zh: PMax 里混着品牌词的 ROAS 是假象——总账漂亮的 400% ROAS 拆开看可能 brand 是 900%、非品牌只有 300%。直接照抄 PMax 的 ROAS 目标到 standard shopping 必死。正确做法:先在 PMax 里 exclude brand 跑一段,brand 流量自然落进新建的 brand standard shopping 和 brand search,这时 PMax 的 ROAS 会从 400 掉到 300,这个 300 才是非品牌真实水平,拿它当新 standard shopping 的目标。Amazon 上同理:Sponsored Brand 把品牌词单拎出来,Sponsored Display 看 non-brand 的真实 ROAS。
quote_en: By excluding the brand we now get a clearer idea of what is our PMax actual ROAS. So when we're setting up standard shopping now, we know okay, standard shopping shouldn't be at 400% ROAS target, it should be at 300%. By this part, we actually know exactly how much our non-brand shopping is supposed to be from a ROAS perspective.

title_zh: PMax→Standard 平滑切换:逐步抬 PMax ROAS 把量挤过来
summary_zh: 首选打法是 gradual overlap:新 standard shopping 建好后两个 campaign 平行跑 1-2 周。这时 PMax 因为有数据优势会吃掉 90% 流量,standard shopping 只有 10%,正常。强行换不靠改预算,靠每周抬 PMax 的 ROAS 目标:第一周双方都 500,然后 PMax 涨到 550、600、700,standard shopping 不动,流量自然往 standard 跑。一到 50/50 就立刻 100% 砍掉 PMax,不要因为'看起来还行'多跑——双 campaign 长跑会割裂数据让两个都变差。Amazon 上 SP→SD 切换/auto→manual 切换思路一样:别一刀切,用预算/出价梯度做平滑迁移。
quote_en: Option A is the gradual campaign overlap, and this is my personal go-to. The best way to do this is by increasing the ROAS target on PMax. So in week one we have a 500% ROAS target on both campaigns. Then PMax goes up to 550, then the following week 600, then 700. Once you're at the 50/50 mark, switch over to standard shopping. Do not attempt fate by letting this run for much longer.

title_zh: branded campaign 是品牌防御不是 funnel 阶段,别叫 prospecting
summary_zh: 把账户结构命名为 branded search / prospecting search 听起来对称,但底层逻辑错了。branded 不是 funnel 的某一层(top/mid/bottom),它是品牌防御位——你必须在,因为竞争对手会买你的词。它不参与拉新转化漏斗,也不是 mid/bottom funnel keyword(除非你像 Nike 那样品牌词流量已经成了大众类目搜索)。把 brand 和 prospecting 并列报表会污染你看 non-brand ROAS 的判断。Amazon 上同理:Sponsored Brand 投自己品牌词主要是防御 ASIN 抢位,不该跟 Sponsored Display 拉新混在一起算 ROAS。
quote_en: Saying you have a campaign that's called prospecting and then you have prospecting search indicates to me that you're looking at branded search as something that is a part of your funnel when it's not. It's just you have to be there. You have to defend the position. It's just a brand defense. So that completely misunderstands the funnel.

title_zh: branded shopping 出现非品牌词,根因是 campaign 优先级设错不是缺 script
summary_zh: 很多 in-house 跑脚本往 branded shopping campaign 里塞非品牌 negative,这是治标。真原因要么是 brand campaign 出价太高,要么是 brand campaign 里的 SKU 没同时出现在 non-brand campaign 里——这时 priority 设置失效,brand 反而吃了非品牌流量。Andrew 跑千万级账户从不用脚本,只调 priority 和确保产品在 non-brand 里都有。一个例外:PMax + standard shopping 同时存在时,优先级会被 PMax 打乱,这时也别加 negative,改架构。Amazon SD 投 ASIN-defense 时同理:看 placement 报表别看到 non-brand 流量就乱加 negative ASIN,先查投放结构。
quote_en: When non-branded terms appear in your branded shopping, it's typically because of one or two reasons. Either your bids are way too high in your branded campaigns, or B, the products in your branded campaigns aren't in the non-branded campaigns. We run multi-million dollar campaigns without them. Using the script is just playing whack-a-mole.

title_zh: category-based campaign segmentation 是错的,只有季节品类才拆
summary_zh: 把 shopping 按 category 拆成 tops vs bottoms 这种 arbitrary split 没有任何性能差异——搜的人不分,不同 category 的 conversion rate 也没差。这种拆法只会减少 smart bidding 的数据量。唯一合理的 category 拆分:有明显季节区分的 apparel(冬季 coat vs 夏季 short),且确实季节切换时整组商品停跑。其他场景一律不拆 category。同样,margin-based segmentation 也是错的,因为 click 不等于购买,改用 profit-based bidding(把 conversion value 换成 gross profit)。Amazon 上 SD/SB 按品类拆 campaign 也要克制——除非品类间转化漏斗真的不同。
quote_en: Product category segmentation, particularly effective for what? I have never in my life seen that work. So this in my opinion is a huge mistake. You should avoid any of these arbitrary category splits like a top versus a bottom. There's no performance difference whatsoever between the two. It just reduces the data volume for smart bidding to do.

title_zh: margin-based campaign 失效是因为点击不等于购买
summary_zh: 把 shopping/PMax 按 margin 拆成高/中/低三档 ROAS 目标(300/500/700%) 听起来很对,实际三处全错:(1) 至少 30-50% 消费者最终买的不是他们点的那个 ASIN/category——bidding 是按点击出价的,可购买行为对不上;(2) Google 的 conversion 报表只追溯到点击的 product id 不是真正卖出的 product id,自己看了个假象;(3) 数据被切到三个 campaign,low margin 那条只剩 150 单/月,smart bidding 直接学不会。结论:不要按 margin 拆 campaign。Amazon 上同样:不要按 ACoS target 把同类目 SKU 拆到不同 campaign,会饿死数据。
quote_en: It assumes the consumers buy what they click on, which isn't the case. I've done this analysis in many, many accounts and every single time I review it, at least 30 to 50% of consumers buy a completely different brand or completely different category than the product that they clicked on.

title_zh: 用 gross profit 替代 revenue 做 conversion value 是 margin 问题正解
summary_zh: 想区分高低 margin 产品,正解不是拆 campaign,是把上传给 Google 的 conversion value 从 revenue 换成 gross profit(= revenue - 折扣 - COGS - 运费 - 支付费 - 包装)。每笔订单的真实利润直接喂给 smart bidding,算法自动对高 margin 产品/词/audience 加价、对低 margin 减价,数据完全保留在一个 campaign 里。注意:促销期 conversion value 会突变(100 跌到 70),smart bidding 会迷惑;低 margin 业务(POAS 120% 那种)profit-based 数据噪音太大也不适合。Amazon 站内拿不到 cogs 喂回去,但思路通用:做内部 PoAS 报表辅助决策,别只看 ACoS。
quote_en: What we're trying to do is we're trying to track the gross profit from a sale instead of revenue, which means that you bypass the need to run margin based campaigns. Each transaction tells Google exactly what margin or contribution margin or gross profit that's derived from that transaction.

title_zh: 每次改 bid/budget 必在 graph 上打 note,3 秒省未来几小时复盘
summary_zh: Andrew 15 年最大习惯之一:任何 bidding/budget/暂停/大改都要在 Google Ads graph 里加一条 3 秒的小注释,同时把决策思路存到 Notion 数据库。日后看到某天 spend 断崖、ROAS 异常就能秒查到原因,而不是凭记忆瞎猜。Amazon SP/SB/SD 同理——后台没有 graph annotation,但可以在飞书表格 / Notion 里建 change log,每次改 bid、target ACoS、加 negative、暂停 ASIN 都记一行。复盘和向老板解释波动时这就是救命数据。
quote_en: When you make a bidding change, when you make a budget change, when you make a bigger change to campaigns, new pausing, a lot of stuff, just put in a small note. It takes you 3 seconds to do. And you can then in the future see, hey, we saw lower spend on this day or after this day, what happened?

title_zh: 主动周报 30 分钟换走 80% 临时打断
summary_zh: in-house 投手最大问题是被动响应——老板/品类经理/品牌部随时插队问数据。Andrew 解法:每周固定花 30 分钟(或 10 分钟 Loom)主动发一封周报,覆盖正在跑的 sale、push 的产品、两周前定的事进展、关键指标变化。看似多花 30 分钟,实际把全周零碎打断削掉 80%。Amazon 投手对接老板/电商运营/品牌方时同理:周一固定时间发周报压住所有'你看到 ACoS 涨了吗'的随机骚扰,把自己的 deep work 时段保护下来。
quote_en: Proactive communication lower all other communication tasks by 80%. Just making sure that you every week you send a report or an update out to your manager. Take those 30 minutes and give them an update on everything. It stops people from asking you a thousand questions randomly throughout the week.

title_zh: feed/listing 拆成 hygiene + optimization 两阶段,90% 工作量是首次 buildout
summary_zh: Andrew 10 年管 Google Shopping feed 的核心方法:把 feed 优化拆成两个独立任务。第一阶段 hygiene 是 pass/fail 黑白题——GTIN/brand/availability/condition 是否填对、是否齐全,一次做完几乎不再碰;第二阶段 optimization 才是 title/image/product type/custom label 这些有判断的活。多数人卡死是因为把两件事混在一起做就崩了。Amazon 上 SP/SB 投放对应的是 listing buildout(GTIN/UPC、品牌、属性是否齐全) vs listing optimization(title 关键词、A+、Main Image),先把 hygiene 100% 补齐再投预算优化转化,不要一上来就调 bid。
quote_en: The key isn't to try to optimize everything. It is to split the work into two distinct jobs. One is the feed hygiene and the other one is what I call the true feed optimization. Hygiene is your foundation. It's a pass or fail. Things like your GTIN and availability, brand color, etc. Your only job here is to be 100% accurate.

title_zh: title 关键词宁愿堆多别堆少,过度优化的 CTR 损失远小于不优化的 visibility 损失
summary_zh: Andrew 见过两种 title 错误:overdoing(brand+title+gender+model+color+size+material 全堆,90% 没人搜) 和 not doing at all。结论是——如果只能选一种错就 overdo,因为堆多了 CTR 顶多掉一点,不优化直接丢整盘 visibility。同时要 frontload 最重要的关键词(reseller 把 brand 放最前,DC 自有品牌放最后或不放),不同行业 model number 关键性也不同(电子/配件搜得多,服饰几乎没人搜)。Amazon SP listing title 同理:堆够核心关键词(品牌 + 品类 + 关键属性 + 使用场景),frontload 高搜索量词,SKU 少时直接用 AI 重写一轮 + 人工 QA。
quote_en: I see two types of mistakes when it comes to optimizing product titles. Number one is overdoing it, and the second mistake is not doing it at all. If you need to make a mistake, if you're worried about overdoing it or underdoing it, overdo it. It has a minimal impact on your CTR if you overdo it, and it has a huge impact on your overall visibility if you don't optimize your titles at all.

title_zh: custom label 用来打 bestseller / saboteur / on-sale 标记后再分组出价
summary_zh: Google Shopping 的 custom labels 跟 visibility/排名零关系——加关键词无意义。Andrew 真正用法:打业务标签做分析和分组出价。最常用三个:(1) bestseller:类目顶尖单品,可以单独提 bid;(2) saboteur:不管怎么调 smart bidding 都亏的产品,直接低出价或排除;(3) on-sale:大促/seasonal 期临时标,方便统一调出价。其他可选:price range(高 AOV 单独看)、margin、delivery time、是否新品。Amazon 上对应的是 portfolio + ASIN-level 标签思路:把 SKU 按 bestseller/dog/促销/新品分组,用不同 ACoS 目标和预算,而不是一刀切跑。
quote_en: Custom labels have nothing to do with visibility. My favorite custom labels are bestseller like categorizing if a product is a bestseller or not and also the opposite if it's what we call a saboteur where it's just it's an unprofitable product no matter what we do from a smart bidding perspective. My other favorite is if it's on sale.

title_zh: 促销期 profit-based bidding 会进入 yo-yo 死循环,bid 越调越乱
summary_zh: profit-based bidding(把 conversion value 从 revenue 换成 profit)在 SKU 间 margin 差异大时是神器,但促销期会反噬。原理:revenue 模式下 100 块 AOV 打 8 折降到 80,只跌 20%;profit 模式下 50 利润降到 30,跌 40%。smart bidding 看不懂这种暴跌就开始降 bid,降完一轮 ROAS 数据反而变好又冲回去抬 bid——CPC 一周高一周低,profit 反而更差。Amazon 上没有原生 profit bidding,但促销期手动管 ACoS 目标时同理:大促时不要按平日 ACoS 标准看波动,目标要拍宽松,出价别频繁追涨跌。
quote_en: When you drop your AOV by 20%, you drop your margins by even more. That's a 40% drop in AOV. This means that smart bidding suddenly can't hit its targets. And during a sale, it will start decreasing bids instead of increasing them. The opposite then happens once it's reduced the bids because now ROAS will look too good and it will start increasing the bids again. So we end up with this yo-yo effect.

title_zh: 想看 profit 不一定要切 bid 模式,secondary conversion + 自定义列即可
summary_zh: 想用 profit 数据有三种用途:(1) 不同 margin SKU 出价分层、(2) 让 Google 端 KPI 对齐业务利润 KPI、(3) 找最高 profit 的 ROAS 甜点。只有 (1) 必须切 profit-based bidding,(2)(3) 完全可以保留 revenue 主转化,把 profit 上传成 secondary conversion,然后用自定义列 (all conversion value - cost) 看 net profit / POAS。这样既不破坏 smart bidding 稳定性又拿到 profit 视角。Amazon 同理:别把 ACoS 改成 net ACoS 模型搞自己,做个外部 PoAS 报表辅助看,主出价目标继续按 ACoS。
quote_en: Just because you want to track your profit, it doesn't mean you have to change your entire account to be bidding for profit. If you're just trying to align Google Ads KPIs with business KPIs or just discover what ROAS target drives the highest profit volume, then you can basically just set the profit conversion action to secondary and observe it instead.

title_zh: smart bidding 三个稳定支柱:数据量 + conversion rate 稳定 + AOV 稳定
summary_zh: Andrew 多年研究 smart bidding 何时高效,得出三个支柱:(1) 大数据量;(2) conversion rate 稳定;(3) AOV 稳定。算法靠这三个量预测某个 bid 下能拿到的 ROAS/CPA,任意一个剧烈波动算法就拍不准。所以频繁促销、SKU 价差极大、AOV 突变的账户 smart bidding 都会变糟。Amazon SP/SD 出价算法本质类似:大促前后、新品冷启动期都属于 conversion rate / AOV 不稳定阶段,这时候不要把出价权全交算法,应该手动稳出价、控预算,等数据稳定后再放手。
quote_en: We found it comes back to three things. Number one is lots of data. The second thing is stability in the conversion rate. And the third thing is stability in your AOV. Smart bidding uses all three to predict what your ROAS or CPA can will be with a specific bid. When one of the three factors are off, then smart bidding has a tough time predicting what the optimal bid is.

title_zh: AI Max 会让高 ROAS campaign 偷偷扩到差词,strict 边界控制必须保留
summary_zh: AI Max 是升级版 DSA,会扫 landing page + 现有 keyword + ad copy 自动扩词。问题:一个原本聚焦 Nike running shoe 的高 ROAS campaign,开了 AI Max 后会扩到 CrossFit shoe / 健身包等历史就不转化的词,而你拦不住——smart bidding 会用高 ROAS 数据给低质词加价,污染整个 campaign 结构。Andrew 的解法:开 AI Max 但关掉 final URL expansion,等于只做关键词扩展不做新着陆页扩展。Amazon 上 SP auto/Sponsored Display 自动定向同理:开就要给清晰的 ASIN/category 边界,定期扫 search term 报表把跑偏的词加 negative,不能信全自动。
quote_en: There's no way for me to say that AI Max will not expand the campaign to show for CrossFit shoes or shoes for weightlifting if those keywords can be found in the URLs that are in the campaign. A high ROAS campaign like Nike will then allow AI Max to bid higher for lower performing search terms that we haven't even added to the campaign.

title_zh: 不开 AI 自动写文案,会自动追高 CTR 低转化的'促销标题'
summary_zh: AI Max 的 text asset generation(让算法基于 landing page + 历史广告自动写新 headline)目前控制力不足。可预见的失败模式:某品类一个 SKU 在打 48% off,AI 给整个 ad group 写了 'Save 48%' 的 headline,因为 CTR 高就吃掉所有 impression 预算,但实际转化率很低(其他 SKU 没这折扣)。Andrew 的建议是不开自动文案。Amazon 上对应的是 Sponsored Brand 的 AI generated headline / AI image,以及自动写 A+ 内容——开之前要预审,确认不会因为某个 SKU 折扣而误导整组商品的预期。
quote_en: I can foresee a scenario where AI Max writes an ad where it says save 48% because one product is on sale in a category. And that headline will then take all the spend because the CTR is the highest but conversion rates will be really low. So in general, I don't recommend turning on text asset generation.

title_zh: 用 wartime/peacetime 框架决定要不要冒险买不可追踪流量
summary_zh: smart bidding 看不见的流量也有价值——top-funnel 词、不直接转化但带后续访问的 click 都不会被算法奖励,所以 smart bidding 会主动放弃。决定要不要补这部分流量看账户阶段:wartime(利润紧张、无容错空间)只买能直接看到回报的流量,smart bidding 默认设置就好;peacetime(利润有 buffer、要扩规模)允许浪费一部分预算去买上层漏斗流量。绝大多数账户实测从 smart bidding 切到完全 manual bidding 反而更差,90% 的实验都不划算。Amazon 上同理——不要在 ACoS 紧张的时候开 SD 上层 audience,只在利润 buffer 够时拿来扩增量。
quote_en: I categorize them into wartime and peacetime. Wartime means that our profitability is key and with no margin of error. Solely spending your budget where it's evident we get a return. Peacetime means that we have headroom in our profitability. We are looking to grow and we can handle some waste. In 90% of the accounts that we test with switching over to manual bidding, smart bidding did better.

title_zh: minimum bid 在 portfolio 里强制 smart bidding 买它学不到价值的流量
summary_zh: smart bidding 只在自己看得见 conversion 的地方加价,看不见的它就放弃出价,你会丢一批本来有价值但无法归因的 click。解法:portfolio bid strategy 里设 minimum bid。一个真实账户实验把 min bid 移除当天 click 量直接腰斩(从 3000+ 跌到一千多),CPC 飙升,但 conversion 数几乎不变——证明那批被压掉的 click 算法无法估值。重新加回 min bid 流量回来。可以只对 bestseller / top campaign 用,搭配 negative keyword 把不相关词剔掉。Amazon 端口对应:bid floor / placement modifier 别一味跟 ACoS 跑,某些有真实增量的展位需要硬出价兜底。
quote_en: Minimum bids basically tell smart bidding to always bid at least X amount. We removed the minimum bid limit just to see what would happen. Literally from one day to another, we actually just dropped massively in amount of clicks. Not that much in the amount of conversions. It basically can't tell that we've made this change in the amount of conversions.

title_zh: smart bidding exploration:让算法用 -10/-30% ROAS 发掘冷门词
summary_zh: Google 把 smart bidding exploration 放出 beta 了,允许 smart bidding 在 10-30% 低于目标 ROAS 的预算口袋里去试它平时不会出价的边缘 search query。例子:卖跑鞋的账户算法默认会买 'running shoes',但不会买 'learning to run' 或 'running shoes for beginners' 这类 less obvious 词;开 exploration 后这些词进入测试。比单纯下调 ROAS target 更精准,因为它只在 exploration 流量上让步,不让全账户 ROAS 滑坡。可以叠加 minimum bid 一起用,防止算法过度追效率。Amazon 上对应做法:Sponsored Display contextual + audience auto-targeting,周期性放宽 ACoS 目标让算法找新词/新 ASIN。
quote_en: It's called smart bidding exploration. It allows smart bidding to hit a 10 to 30% lower ROAS while exploring new search terms that you're currently not bidding for. The system knows that if we're selling running shoes, it should be bidding for running shoes. But what about the term 'learning to run' or 'first-time runner' or 'running shoes for beginners'? Combining smart bidding exploration with minimum bids can together be a great way.
